PRASHUK

Prashuk Jain—AI & Systems Engineer.

I build high-throughput training frameworks, custom GPU kernels, and low-latency inference runtimes. Focused on making large models fast, cheap, and scalable.

Profile

Bridging ML research and hardware.

I spend most of my time writing custom Triton kernels, optimizing communication primitives (NCCL), and solving cluster memory bottlenecks. I help startups design and scale their training runtimes to reduce cloud compute bills.

My work centers on pragmatism: reducing CUDA memory overhead, designing parallel execution graphs, and writing robust infrastructure that handles multi-node hardware drops gracefully.

Details & Core Metrics

Project Case Studies.

01 // DISTRIBUTED RUNTIME

NeuroForge Engine

A distributed training extension that speeds up multi-node transformer training by 3×. Features gradient quantization and async overlap between communication and backward propagation.

Stack: PyTorch · Triton · CUDA · NCCL
02 // CLUSTER TELEMETRY

Argus Observability

Real-time sharded tracing platform measuring pipeline latency and parameter drift at 50,000 requests per second across large model clusters.

Stack: C++ · Rust · Kafka · ClickHouse
03 // SYMBOLIC RETRIEVAL

Semantic Shell

A RAG engine featuring a customized virtual memory AST tree parser. Eliminates reference errors and limits hallucinations in complex workspace scans.

Stack: C++ · Triton · AST · pgvector
History

Professional Timeline

2023 — Present

Senior Systems Architect

Stealth AI Startup, San Francisco

Scaling model parallel training platforms. Designed custom kernel pipelines reducing model memory print by 35% on A100 architectures. Integrated customized RAG pipelines.

2021 — 2023

Machine Learning Engineer

Meridian Labs (acquired 2023)

Built real-time telemetry processing 50k events/sec. Decreased model drift reaction cycle from 4 hours to under 10 minutes. Integrated high-performance data visualization grids.

2020 — 2021

ML Infrastructure Engineer

NLP Lab, University of California

Built linear memory attention runtimes. Speed up deep networks throughput while preserving context windows. Maintained lab compute systems and GPU clusters.
